methods.method_name: Pollen Sample Collection and Initial Preparation
methods.description: Procedure for selecting herbarium specimens and collecting pollen from anthers for subsequent analysis, ensuring sample quality and representativeness.

steps.description: pollen of all samples were collected from exsiccate deposited in public herbaria (vouchers listed below).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect pollen samples from herbarium specimens.

steps.description: anthers from pre-anthesis buds were utilized only, since this procedure avoids contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: Select anthers from pre-anthesis buds for pollen extraction.

steps.description: One to five (seven in Hortia brasiliana) different specimen to each species were studied.
steps.searchable_summary: Select multiple specimens per species for study.
outputs: List of specimens selected for study

steps.description: one specimen of each species was chosen to the descriptions and photographs ("reference material").
steps.searchable_summary: Designate a reference specimen for each species.
inputs: List of specimens selected for study

steps.description: pollen of H. neblinensis, known only from its type collection (with very young buds) were observed in light microscopy (lM) only, but the grains were neither measured nor photographed, since they always collapsed after acetolysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Handle H. neblinensis pollen as a special case for LM observation only, excluding measurement and photography.
outputs: H. neblinensis pollen sample designated for limited analysis

methods.description: Chemical treatment of pollen grains to remove cytoplasm and sporopollenin, followed by mounting on glass slides for microscopy.

steps.description: pollen grains were acetolysed according to the method described by erdtman (1960)
steps.searchable_summary: Perform pollen acetolysis using the method described by Erdtman (1960).

steps.description: and then mounted in glycerine jelly on 3-5 glass slides to each specimen.
steps.searchable_summary: Mount acetolysed pollen grains on glass slides using glycerine jelly.

methods.description: Procedure for observing and measuring morphological characteristics of pollen grains using a light microscope, including size, shape, apertures, and exine thickness.

steps.description: Grains were observed and measured in an olympus microscope with a ruler adapted to the ocular lens.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe and measure pollen grains using light microscopy with an ocular ruler.

steps.description: the measurements were taken in a maximum of one-week period because the frequent inflating of the grains after some days, as reported in Melhem &Matos (1972) andsalgado-labouriau (1973).
steps.searchable_summary: Complete measurements within one week of mounting to avoid pollen inflation.

steps.description: Measurements of both polar (p) and equatorial (e) diameters were taken in 25 pollen grains in the reference material.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure polar (P) and equatorial (E) diameters for 25 pollen grains from the reference material.
outputs: P and E diameter measurements (reference material)

steps.description: other measurements, such as to apertures, endoapertures and thickness of the exine were taken in just 10 grains.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure apertures, endoapertures, and exine thickness for 10 pollen grains from the reference material.
outputs: Aperture, endoaperture, exine thickness measurements (reference material)

steps.description: the remnant material of each species (the "comparison material") had taken just 10 measures of p and e diameters.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure polar (P) and equatorial (E) diameters for 10 pollen grains from the comparison material.
outputs: P and E diameter measurements (comparison material)

steps.description: Measurements in the "reference" and "comparison" materials were used to define shape and size classes of pollen, according to definitions of Barth & Melhem (1988).
steps.searchable_summary: Classify pollen shape and size based on measurements using criteria from Barth & Melhem (1988).
inputs: P and E diameter measurements (reference and comparison)

steps.description: photomicrographs of the grains in lM were obtained with an olympus video camera connected to a personal Computer.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture photomicrographs of pollen grains using LM with a video camera.

methods.description: Procedure for preparing and analyzing pollen grains using a scanning electron microscope to study surface ornamentation and aperture details.

steps.description: scanning electron Microscopy (seM) analyses were made using grains collected directly from dry anthers or from acetolysed grains stocked in glycerine.
steps.searchable_summary: Select pollen samples for SEM analysis from dry anthers or acetolysed preparations.

steps.description: the acetolysed grains were washed in distilled water and dehydrated in an alcoholic series (50%, 70% 90% and absolute alcohol).
steps.searchable_summary: Wash and dehydrate acetolysed pollen samples using an ethanol series.

steps.description: pollen samples (non and acetolysed grains) were subsequently mounted on stubs
steps.searchable_summary: Mount pollen samples (dry or dehydrated acetolysed) on SEM stubs.
inputs: Prepared pollen samples (dry or dehydrated acetolysed)

steps.description: and sputtered with gold.
steps.searchable_summary: Sputter coat mounted SEM samples with gold.

steps.description: electromicrographs were obtained from a Jeol JsM 840a-scanning Microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire SEM images (electromicrographs) using a Jeol JSM 840A scanning microscope.

steps.description: Images in SEM were used to define the pattern of ornamentation of exine and apertures, according to the terminology of Barth
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze SEM images to describe exine ornamentation and aperture patterns using Barth terminology.
outputs: Description of exine ornamentation and aperture patterns
